title: Convert legacy module syntax to Move 2024 file-level form
prompt: Update sources/legacy.move to use the Move 2024 file-level module syntax. Do not change function bodies; only change the module declaration form.

title: Migrate to @mysten/sui v2 JSON-RPC client
prompt: Update src/index.ts to use the @mysten/sui v2 SDK. The 1.x SuiClient is deprecated; switch to SuiJsonRpcClient from @mysten/sui/jsonRpc and pass the now-required network parameter (mainnet).

title: Implement the one-time-witness pattern for a currency module
prompt: Implement the one-time-witness (OTW) pattern in sources/demo.move so the coin::create_currency call in init() compiles. The OTW struct must match the module name uppercased (DEMO) and have only the drop ability. Pass the OTW by value into init.

title: Use Move 2024 method-call syntax for vector ops
prompt: Update sources/lib.move to use Move 2024 method-call syntax (`v.push_back(x)`, `v.pop_back()`) instead of the function-call form. Behaviour must be identical.

title: Replace a while loop with the vector::do! macro
prompt: Rewrite the summation loop in sources/lib.move using the Move 2024 `vector::do!` macro instead of a `while` loop.

title: Attach and read a dynamic object field on a Parent
prompt: Add two public functions to sources/lib.move: `add_child(parent: &mut Parent, key: u64, child: Child)` using `dynamic_object_field::add`, and `borrow_child(parent: &Parent, key: u64): &Child` using `dynamic_object_field::borrow`. Define a simple `Child` struct with `key + store` abilities.

title: Drop explicit Sui framework dep in favour of implicit (1.45+)
prompt: Update Move.toml to use Sui 1.45+ implicit framework dependencies — remove the explicit `Sui = { ... }` line entirely. Edition stays at 2024.

title: Apply the hot-potato pattern to a Receipt struct
prompt: Make the trade flow safe: ensure `Receipt` has NO abilities (hot-potato pattern), and add a `complete_trade(receipt: Receipt, ...)` function that destroys the receipt by-value via destructuring.

title: Create a TransferPolicy with a 5% royalty rule
prompt: Add a function `init_policy(publisher: &Publisher, ctx: &mut TxContext)` that creates a `TransferPolicy<MyNft>` via `transfer_policy::new`, attaches a 5% royalty rule, and shares the policy.

title: Write a multi-actor test_scenario test for a shared Pool
prompt: Write a `tests/pool_tests.move` with a `#[test]` function that uses `test_scenario` to: create the shared Pool, have actor ALICE deposit, then have actor BOB withdraw concurrently. Use `test_scenario::next_tx` between actors and `test_scenario::take_shared`.

title: Mint a Child via sui::derived_object
prompt: Add a `derive_child(parent: &mut Parent, index: u64, ctx: &mut TxContext)` function that uses `sui::derived_object` to create a `Child` with a deterministic UID derived from the parent's UID and the index.

title: Pick a random raffle winner with sui::random
prompt: Implement a `draw_winner(raffle: &mut Raffle, r: &Random, ctx: &mut TxContext)` function that uses `sui::random::Random` to pick a random index in the participants vector. Use `random::new_generator(r, ctx)` and `random_generator::generate_u64_in_range`.

title: Publish a blob to Walrus and anchor its id on Sui
prompt: In src/index.ts, implement `publishAndAnchor(blob: Uint8Array): Promise<{ blobId: string; suiObjectId: string }>` that writes the blob to Walrus via `WalrusClient.writeBlob`, then submits a Sui transaction creating a `BlobCommitment` object containing the returned blobId. Use the v2 SDK shape.

title: Encrypt a secret with a Seal capability-gated policy
prompt: In src/index.ts, implement `encryptForCapability(secret: Uint8Array, capabilityId: string): Promise<Uint8Array>` that uses `@mysten/seal`'s SealClient to encrypt the secret with a capability-gated decryption policy referencing the given capabilityId.

title: Refactor a u8-tagged state machine to a Move 2024 enum + match
prompt: Refactor sources/state.move to replace the `u8` tag with a Move 2024 `enum State` (variants `Idle`, `Running`, `Done`), and rewrite `step` to use exhaustive `match` over the variants.
